text.title: Monthly Billing Batch Routine Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Monthly Billing Batch Routine methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Batch fires on a fixed monthly anchor (e.g. 1st business day) every active month; missed months MUST be acknowledged with reason, never silently skipped.
rule.rationale: skipping a month silently corrupts the ledger and the founder loses cash-flow visibility.

rule.statement: Each batch carries BOTH customer_invoices[] AND contractor_payouts[]; emitting one without the other is rejected.
rule.rationale: decoupling the two sides hides margin compression; the routine exists to surface it monthly.

rule.statement: Every non-zero customer invoice line AND every contractor payout MUST cite a source (timesheet, deliverable, SoW clause); un-anchored lines are rejected.
rule.rationale: agent-driven batching invents plausible amounts; anchoring keeps it auditable.

rule.statement: Margin row (invoices - payouts) MUST be computed by the script from the line items, not accepted as agent-supplied input; manual override requires founder sign-off and a comment.
rule.rationale: agent-supplied margin smooths over real compression; force the math from line items.

rule.statement: Batch status approved_to_send MUST come from the founder (named reviewer), never the agent; auto-approval is forbidden.
rule.rationale: self-approval defeats the human checkpoint and the audit trail.

rule.statement: Batch MUST include a prior_month_delta block reconciling outstanding invoices and unpaid payouts from the prior month; gaps without delta are rejected.
rule.rationale: without prior-month delta the founder loses visibility into rolling A/R and A/P.
